2606.19727.title: NRITYAM: Language Models Meet Art and Heritage of Dance
2606.19727.paper_summary: Language models have become essential tools in shaping modern workflows. However, their global effectiveness hinges on a nuanced understanding of local socio-cultural contexts. To address this gap, we present NRITYAM, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating the cultural comprehension capabilities of language models in the context of global dance traditions. NRITYAM comprises 9,260 carefully curated question-answer pairs spanning 12 languages, making it the largest dataset dedicated to evaluating cultural knowledge in dance. The dataset has been developed from the ground up through close collaboration with native dance artists and native speakers of the languages, who authored and validated culturally relevant questions specific to their regions. We evaluate a broad set of models, including large language models, small language models, multimodal large language models, and small multimodal language models. As a multilingual and multicultural benchmark, NRITYAM sets a new standard for evaluating the ability of AI systems to understand and reason about traditional performing arts. Detailed dataset samples are available at~\url{https://github.com/niladrighosh03/NRITYAM}.
2606.19727.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型已成為塑造現代工作流程的重要工具。然而，它們的全球有效性取決於對當地社會文化背景的細緻理解。為了解決這一差距，我們提出了NRITYAM，這是一個全面的基準，用於評估語言模型在全球舞蹈傳統背景下的文化理解能力。NRITYAM包含9,260對精心策劃的問答對，涵蓋12種語言，使其成為專門用於評估舞蹈文化知識的最大數據集。該數據集是通過與當地舞蹈藝術家和語言母語者的密切合作從零開始開發的，他們撰寫並驗證了特定於其地區的文化相關問題。我們評估了一系列廣泛的模型，包括大型語言模型、小型語言模型、多模態大型語言模型和小型多模態語言模型。作為一個多語言和多文化的基準，NRITYAM為評估人工智能系統理解和推理傳統表演藝術的能力設立了新的標準。詳細的數據集樣本可在~\url{https://github.com/niladrighosh03/NRITYAM}獲得。
2606.19727.author: Punit Kumar Singh et.al.
2606.19727.authors: Punit Kumar Singh, Niladri Ghosh, Advait Joshiınst, Shailee Choudhary, Michael Färber, Haiqin Yang
